notes: First validated clue batch compiled from official school websites and official school documents.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: This school's vision says, "Inspire every Viking to learn, innovate, and thrive."
Its vision statement highlights project-based learning in a safe, inclusive, and supportive environment.
This charter was established in 2007, and its first graduating class was in 2014.
Students here can earn Career and College Promise credit through Durham Technical Community College.
Its arts accomplishments include NCASA state show choir titles for "Bull City Lights and Sound."
Its athletics page highlights a 2019 men's soccer state championship.
Peter Meath is listed as chair of this school's board of directors.
Its admissions materials describe the school as a rigorous, experiential, and challenging public charter option.
Its mission is to increase access to globally competitive STEM education by incubating innovative models of teaching and learning.
Official materials say this school blends content learning, projects, and mentoring into its curriculum.
Its school profile says flipped classrooms mix teacher-created lessons with curated content from around the world.
The school says its graduates are primed for college completion and careers in RTP.
Akiba Griffin is listed as the Chief School Officer in the 2025-26 school profile.
Its handbook says personalized learning includes student agency, flexible instruction, and performance-based assessments.
Admissions materials describe this school as focused on giving students access to competitive STEM opportunities across North Carolina.
Its mission is to prepare students for college, career, and purposeful lives.
This school was authorized by the North Carolina State Board of Education in August 2006.
Its homepage highlights focused programming in Fine Arts, STEM, and Spanish.
The school says signature traditions include butterfly releases and senior parades.
Its athletics page says the school has won or been a finalist in the Wells Fargo Cup since 2020.
Andrew Moceri serves as the Executive Director.
The most recent graduating class highlighted by the Executive Director was awarded more than 11 million dollars in merit-based scholarships.
The campus is located on Yellow Wood Circle in Mooresville.

schools.officialName: Francine Delany New School for Children

clues.text: Its homepage describes the school as a diverse community committed to social justice and the inherent worth of every person.
This school describes itself as Asheville's first charter school.
Its history page says the school was started by seven teachers and three families.
Before its campus was ready, the school operated for a little over a month inside New Mount Olive Baptist Church.
October 19 is celebrated there as the school's spiritual anniversary.
The school was named after a local educator and principal who had worked with many of its founders.
Ashley Wilberding is listed as board president for the 2025-2026 school year.
Its academic materials emphasize experientially rich, hands-on learning and critical thinking.
This school calls itself the Home of the Navigators.
Its culture is organized around the R.A.I.S.E. values: Respect, Accountability, Integrity, Service, and Excellence.
Its mission begins with the phrase, "In support of the family..."
The school says authentic learning experiences and character development sit at the center of its model.
Glenn Way is identified as the founder of American Leadership Academy.
Amanda Ahlert is listed as Director 7-12 and the website says she has been part of the team since its opening in 2017.
Its website says Charter One supports the school by handling administrative and business duties.
The school publishes a 2025 performance grade of 62%, marked as a C.
